refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall introduce latency and control overhead ≤ acceptable_delta_ct to preserve cycle time target.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time beyond safety-justified margins.
FR-NGHE-002.4: Telemetry shall capture cycle time events (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end) with sufficient fidelity for p95 analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for minimum cycle time; implement predictive load balancing and parallel operation of dump and travel phases where safe.
DP-B: Implement real-time cycle time monitoring via telemetry; use feedback control to adjust operational parameters (e.g., speed profiles, dwell times) to meet p95 target.
DP-C: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; design each to meet allocated sub-targets with margin; integrate via orchestration layer.
DP-D: Combine DP-A (vehicle optimization) + DP-B (telemetry feedback) + DP-C (budget allocation) in an integrated approach with safety-aware cycle time management.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Integrated cycle time management combining vehicle subsystem optimization (load/travel/dump parallelization where safe), real-time telemetry-based monitoring and feedback control, and explicit cycle time budget allocation across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services with safety-aware orchestration to ensure p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target without compromising safety interlocks.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time optimization (DP-A) may conflict with safety service latency requirements if safety checks are not parallelized; mitigation: design safety checks to run concurrently with load/travel phases.
Teleoperation latency (DP-B feedback loop) may introduce oscillation if control gains are not tuned; mitigation: closed-loop stability analysis and adaptive gain scheduling.
Budget allocation (DP-C) requires clear ownership and interface contracts; mitigation: explicit FR→DP→IM traceability at next decomposition level.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composite solution that addresses the KPI requirement holistically. Decoupling is achieved through explicit budget allocation and interface contracts. Safety criticality is preserved by ensuring safety services are not on the critical path for cycle time.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (hydraulic flow rates, mechanical limits). Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it; mitigation: validate CT_target against vehicle physics early.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure can capture cycle events with <100ms resolution. Risk: Coarse telemetry may mask p95 tail behavior; mitigation: high-fidelity event logging.

Assumption: Safety services can be designed to run in parallel with cycle operations. Risk: Some safety checks may be blocking (e.g., load stability verification before travel); mitigation: identify blocking vs. non-blocking safety checks and design accordingly.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency is predictable and bounded. Risk: Network jitter may cause unpredictable delays; mitigation: implement deterministic scheduling and latency budgeting.
Risk: High safety criticality may impose additional validation/certification overhead that delays cycle time optimization; mitigation: integrate safety and performance optimization from the start.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but requires decomposition into sub-DPs for vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services at the next level. Recommend explicit cycle time budget allocation (e.g., vehicle ≤ 80% of CT_target, teleoperation ≤ 15%, safety margin ≤ 5%) to guide sub-system design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and safety-aware orchestration logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time events (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end) and performance metrics (p95 cycle time, latency, safety events) reported to external monitoring/analytics systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Cycle commands (load, travel, dump) and operational parameters (speed profiles, dwell times) received from site manager or tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraints and alerts from safety services; cycle time orchestration respects safety interlocks and adjusts parameters based on safety state.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication with teleoperation subsystem for remote control and with safety services for real-time safety state synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system module and is the natural owner of cycle time KPI orchestration. Cycle time management logic (budget allocation, feedback control, safety-aware scheduling) is best contained here. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry and tasking interfaces cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns the mechanical/hydraulic subsystems that execute load/travel/dump cycles. However, cycle time optimization requires coordination with teleoperation and safety services, which are outside Vehicle scope. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle alone would create high diffusion (teleoperation and safety logic would need to be distributed across multiple modules).

Teleoperation owns remote control and latency management but does not own vehicle mechanics or safety services. Assigning DP-D to Teleoperation would require it to orchestrate vehicle and safety, which is outside its scope.

Safety Services owns safety interlocks and alerts but should not own cycle time optimization logic. Assigning DP-D to Safety Services would create confusion between safety and performance objectives and high diffusion across vehicle and teleoperation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of DP-D (integrated cycle time management) because it sits at the system integration level and can orchestrate vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services without diffusion. Cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and safety-aware orchestration are system-level concerns that belong at the top level.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if too much logic is concentrated there. Mitigation: Decompose DP-D into sub-DPs for vehicle cycle optimization, teleoperation latency management, and safety-aware scheduling; assign sub-DPs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services respectively, with NGHE Solution owning only the orchestration and budget allocation logic.

Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if cycle time events are not efficiently serialized. Mitigation: Design telemetry schema to be compact and asynchronous; use buffering to avoid blocking cycle operations.
Risk: SafetyAlert interface may introduce latency if safety state synchronization is not real-time. Mitigation: Implement deterministic safety state machine with bounded latency guarantees.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign DP-D to NGHE Solution at this level, but plan to decompose at the next level into sub-DPs for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Explicitly define cycle time budget allocation (e.g., vehicle ≤ 80%, teleoperation ≤ 15%, safety margin ≤ 5%) as a design constraint to guide sub-system decomposition.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-002 clearly specifies the cycle time KPI (p95 ≤ CT_target) with measurement and duty cycle context. Selected DP-D (integrated cycle time management) directly addresses the FR by combining vehicle optimization, telemetry feedback, and budget allocation. The DP is feasible and does not introduce unnecessary complexity at this level. Coupling check passes with identified mitigations for safety/performance trade-offs.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Cycle time optimization logic may be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if sub-DPs are not clearly allocated at the next level. Mitigation: Explicit sub-DP allocation and interface contracts.
Diffusion risk: Telemetry aggregation may require changes to multiple modules if cycle time events are not standardized. Mitigation: Define telemetry schema and event types at the system level.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of DP-D with a containment score of 95. The module boundary is clear: NGHE Solution owns orchestration and budget allocation; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own sub-system implementations. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not create hidden dependencies.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-002 is feasible via instrumented field trials with telemetry-based p95 cycle time measurement. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) are measurable and objective. Safety correlation analysis can be performed to ensure no safety compromise.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of DP-D to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-allocations to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services will be defined at the next decomposition level. Cycle time budget allocation (vehicle ≤ 80%, teleoperation ≤ 15%, safety margin ≤ 5%) provides clear targets for sub-system design.

recommended_revisions: Refine CT_target validation: Confirm that CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (hydraulic flow rates, mechanical limits, safety margins) before proceeding to detailed design.
Define telemetry schema: Specify cycle time event types, timestamps, and resolution to ensure p95 analysis is accurate.
Allocate cycle time budget: Explicitly allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services with margin for contingency.
Plan safety-aware orchestration: Design the orchestration logic to respect safety interlocks and avoid blocking delays on the critical path.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs for vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. The refined FR is clear and the selected DP-D is sound, but it is not atomic and requires further decomposition to assign ownership and design details to sub-systems. Proceeding to the next level will clarify how each sub-system contributes to the cycle time KPI and identify potential conflicts or trade-offs early.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope (target: ≤ 80% of CT_target).
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall introduce latency and control overhead ≤ acceptable_delta_ct (target: ≤ 15% of CT_target) to preserve cycle time target.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time beyond safety-justified margins (target: ≤ 5% of CT_target).
FR-NGHE-002.4: Telemetry shall capture cycle time events (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end) with sufficient fidelity (≤100ms resolution) for p95 analysis.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (hydraulic flow rates, mechanical limits, safety margins).
Telemetry infrastructure can capture cycle events with <100ms resolution.
Safety services can be designed to run in parallel with cycle operations (non-blocking checks).
Teleoperation latency is predictable and bounded.
High safety criticality does not impose blocking validation/certification overhead that prevents cycle time optimization.
open_risks: CT_target may be unrealistic; validation needed early.
Telemetry coarse resolution may mask p95 tail behavior.
Some safety checks may be blocking (e.g., load stability verification); design trade-off needed.
Network jitter may cause unpredictable teleoperation latency.
Safety criticality may impose additional validation overhead.
evidence_needed: Vehicle physics analysis confirming CT_target achievability.
Telemetry system design with event resolution and latency specifications.
Safety analysis identifying blocking vs. non-blocking checks.
Teleoperation latency budget and network jitter characterization.
Safety certification requirements and timeline impact assessment.
